name: Naloxone
indications:
## Main indications

Antagonist of opioids and derivatives.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: 0,04–0,4 mg IV every 2–3 min until response (per protocol maximum).

pediatrico.dose: Partial reversal: Newborns: 0,01 mg/kg (maximum dose: 0,1 mg) every 2-3 minutes until response; older children: 0,01 mg/kg (maximum dose: 0,2 mg), Adults: 0,4-2 mg every 2-3 minutes if needed. Continuous infusion: 2,5-160 µg/kg/hour. Complete reversal: 0,1 mg/kg; if no response may repeat every 2-3 min if IV or every 10 min if IM, maximum dose: 2 mg Toxicology: IV 0,01 mg/kg, maximum dose: 0,1 mg/kg; continuous infusion: 0,4 mg/hour.
pediatrico.notes: Must be used by physicians trained in its use. Does not cause respiratory depression, drug dependence, or withdrawal syndrome.

neonatal.dose: 0,1 mg/kg IV/IO if depression due to maternal opioids.

stability:
## General

- Ready to use.

## Pediatric guide

- Discard remainder once opened.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

In overdose: nausea and vomiting (rarely).
